plugins.name: code-reviewer
plugins.description: A subagent that reviews code against the user's conventions and craftsmanship standards

plugins.name: embedded-safety-auditor
plugins.description: A subagent that audits C/C++ embedded code for undefined behavior, concurrency hazards, and resource-safety defects

plugins.name: docs-writer
plugins.description: A subagent that writes and updates code documentation (Doxygen, JSDoc/TSDoc) and READMEs on request

plugins.name: git-historian
plugins.description: A read-only subagent that investigates git history — when/why code changed, who owns an area, and changelog/release notes from a commit range

plugins.name: refactor
plugins.description: A skill that refactors code to match the user's preferred style

plugins.name: tooling-reminder
plugins.description: A UserPromptSubmit hook that reminds Claude to check for a relevant agent, skill, or hook on every request

plugins.name: usetdd
plugins.description: A skill that drives the RED-GREEN-REFACTOR cycle inline — failing test first, minimum code to green, then refactor

plugins.name: interview
plugins.description: A skill that interviews the user about the topic at hand — one focused question at a time — to draw out requirements, decisions, and reasoning

plugins.name: council
plugins.description: A skill that spawns five agents, each arguing a fixed lens (simplicity, risk, cost-benefit, long-term maintenance, contrarian), then reports where they agree and disagree
